Hey, what's going on everyone? Before we turn on the machine, I just wanted to explain what the offers we are making are. You should be relatively aware at this point, just based on the sales process. They should explain that to you. If they did not, here's a brief kind of go over for you. So the platform, most of the time, is going to be Instagram and Facebook, primarily Facebook for now. But the platforms that we'll have in the trainings will be the one that we believe are getting the best on average cost per lead or really cost per sale. So there are multiple headlines that we have tested that have been extremely successful. The six-week challenge or the free six-week challenge is one of them. We no longer do the free six-week challenge or at least many times, the reason it's called the old offer is that Facebook at least flagged those words, but together, free six-week challenge. And so we have since then adapted to a free 42-day challenge and a free 42-day detox or 42-day detox 42-day challenge without the word free, free 42-day transformation or 42-day transformation. Ultimately, the formula for these types of headlines and promotions is going to be the duration, the benefit, and then power it. So it's like 42-day bikini blueprint, six-week metabolic reset, 28-day transformation jump start. So those are all examples of headline formulas. It's very easy. Six-week mommy makeover challenge. It's going to be like there's a million ways you can cut this thing. So we're going to give you the headlines that up to this point have tested very well. Feel free to flex your muscles after you have already run the ones that we have recommended you start with. So this is just to give you a little bit of a primer. We will have different ads with different offers. They all fundamentally work the same way in that whether you're running a 42-day detox, 42-day transformation, six-week challenge, all of those are still going to fundamentally follow the exact same conversion process, which is when they come in the door or so you're going to they will schedule themselves, will confirm it, continue to remind them, reach out, and then when they show up, we are going to sell them a program that is 42 days in length or six weeks in length. And whether it's a challenge of transformation, a detox, etc., all of those ultimately are going to be the same thing, which is we're going to help them work out and we're going to help them change their food. So don't get too caught up in the minutiae or the details of what the actual widget is that we were offering on the front end. What matters is mostly that it's a converting offer and then the fulfillment, it's really just different wrapping paper on the same gift of fitness and nutrition. So just an overall chain explanation there. And then for those of you who are always curious about free or not free, I should probably just make a little slide for that because it will be prettier for everyone. So free versus not free. Essentially, free is going to be, there's multiple ways for someone to earn it for free. One is if they hit the goal, they would be able to get the money back. That is option one. Option two is that if for any reason during the six weeks or 42 days they're with you, they do not feel like they're getting the level of service that they were originally told they would get, then you give them a satisfaction guarantee. I just want to be clear here. I sold everything that we've ever sold with a satisfaction guarantee at my gyms and I had two people ever take me up on it. So if you do your best to fulfill like crazy, people will respond to that. And then lastly, non-free. Non-free works fine. There's obviously going to be slightly higher lead costs but no one's coming in with any kind of free expectation. For the free ads, when you do run free, there is a slightly different funnel, meaning the landing page and the thank you page, actually specifically the thank you page, are going to have just kind of a little bit more explanation on why it is free so that you can be compliant advertisers and not get into any trouble. So that is more or less the free versus non-free discussion. You can be 100% a compliant advertiser by explaining how the different ways they can get the offer for free and ultimately why you are marking it that way. So hopefully that makes sense and let's turn the machine on. Let's dig it.
